skill.name: security-scanning-security-hardening
skill.description: Coordinate multi-layer security scanning and hardening across application, infrastructure, and compliance controls.
skill.summary: Orchestrate comprehensive security scanning, vulnerability remediation, and defense-in-depth hardening across application, infrastructure, and compliance domains.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 11 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate defensive security orchestration tool for DevSecOps workflows. References to Metasploit, CIS Benchmarks, AES-256, and mobile security terms are all standard security industry practices. No malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Metasploit Framework Reference
high_findings.description: Line 106 references 'Metasploit' in penetration testing context. This is a standard authorized security testing tool used in legitimate security assessments.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - legitimate penetration testing tool referenced in authorized security testing workflow

high_findings.title: CIS Benchmarks Misidentified as C2 Keywords
high_findings.description: Lines 112 and 124 contain 'CIS Benchmarks' (Center for Internet Security) - a legitimate compliance framework, not command and control (C2) malware.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - scanner misidentified 'CIS' as C2 (command and control). CIS Benchmarks are industry-standard security configuration guidelines.

high_findings.title: AES-256 Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Lines 3, 52, 53, 60, and 142 reference 'AES-256' which is a strong cryptographic algorithm (not weak).
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - AES-256 is an industry-standard strong encryption algorithm. Scanner incorrectly flagged the 'AES' pattern.

high_findings.title: Mobile Security Terms Flagged as Jailbreak Keywords
high_findings.description: Line 78 references 'root/jailbreak detection' which is legitimate mobile app security hardening, not a jailbreak attempt.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - root/jailbreak detection is a standard defensive security measure for protecting mobile applications.

high_findings.title: Secure Local Storage Reference
high_findings.description: Line 78 references 'secure local storage with encryption' for mobile apps - legitimate security hardening guidance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - this is standard mobile security best practice for protecting data at rest.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Reference
low_findings.description: Line 33 contains 'Avoid intrusive testing in production without approval' - defensive security guidance, not reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - this is security best practice advising against unauthorized testing, not an attack directive.

content.value_statement: Orchestrate comprehensive security scanning, vulnerability remediation, and defense-in-depth hardening across your application, infrastructure, and compliance controls with automated DevSecOps workflows.
actual_capabilities: Coordinate multi-phase security assessments using SAST, DAST, and dependency scanning tools
Implement vulnerability remediation with prioritized fixes based on CVSS scores
Deploy defense-in-depth controls across frontend, backend, and infrastructure layers
Configure authentication and authorization with OAuth2, MFA, and RBAC
Validate compliance against OWASP ASVS, CIS Benchmarks, SOC2, and other frameworks
Establish security monitoring with SIEM integration and automated incident response
limitations: Does not perform actual security testing - only coordinates agents that execute scans
Requires existing authorization and approval for security testing activities
Cannot access external scanning tools or penetration testing services directly
Compliance validation provides guidance but does not replace formal audits

use_cases.title: DevSecOps Security Automation
use_cases.description: Integrate automated security scanning and hardening into CI/CD pipelines to catch vulnerabilities early and maintain continuous security posture.

use_cases.title: Application Security Hardening
use_cases.description: Apply comprehensive security controls to applications including input validation, encryption, authentication hardening, and secure configuration.

use_cases.title: Compliance Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare for security compliance audits by implementing controls and generating documentation for OWASP, SOC2, CIS, and other frameworks.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Security Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to perform a quick vulnerability scan on my application. Focus on identifying critical issues and provide a prioritized remediation list.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial security check for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Security Hardening
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to implement comprehensive security hardening for my application. Include vulnerability assessment, backend security controls, frontend security measures, and compliance validation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full security implementation for production application

prompt_templates.title: Compliance Framework Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to validate my application against OWASP ASVS Level 2 and CIS Benchmarks. Generate a compliance gap analysis and remediation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Incident Response Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-scanning-security-hardening skill to establish security monitoring and SIEM integration for my infrastructure. Configure automated incident response playbooks for critical security events.

output_examples.input: Perform security hardening on my API service
output_examples.output: Security hardening plan: Phase 1 - Vulnerability scanning completed. Found 3 critical SQL injection vulnerabilities. Phase 2 - Applied parameterized queries fix. Phase 3 - Implemented OAuth2 authentication, rate limiting, and AES-256 encryption. Phase 4 - Validated against OWASP Top 10, zero findings remaining.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare for SOC2 compliance
output_examples.output: SOC2 compliance preparation: Implemented access controls with MFA, encrypted data at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.3, configured security monitoring with ELK stack, established incident response procedures. Gap analysis shows 85% compliance readiness.

best_practices: Run security assessments in non-production environments first before applying changes to production
Always maintain rollback plans when implementing security hardening changes
Prioritize remediation based on CVSS scores and business impact, not just automated findings
anti_patterns: Do not skip authorization checks - always verify you have permission before running security scans
Do not apply security changes without testing in a staging environment first
Do not rely solely on automated scanning - combine with manual security review for critical systems

faq.question: Does this skill perform actual penetration testing?
faq.answer: No, this skill orchestrates security workflows and coordinates agents. Actual penetration testing requires licensed tools and authorized testers.

faq.question: Can this skill scan external systems without authorization?
faq.answer: No, the skill includes guidance to avoid intrusive testing without approval. Always ensure you have explicit authorization before scanning any system.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports validation against OWASP ASVS, CIS Benchmarks, SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill require any specific tools to be installed?
faq.answer: The skill coordinates AI agents that may use tools like Semgrep, OWASP ZAP, Snyk, or GitLeaks for scanning. Tool availability depends on your environment.

faq.question: Can this skill break my production application?
faq.answer: Security hardening changes can impact functionality. Always test in non-production environments first and maintain rollback plans as recommended in the skill.

faq.question: How long does a comprehensive security hardening take?
faq.answer: The skill supports quick, standard, and comprehensive scanning modes. Comprehensive hardening across all phases may take hours to days depending on application complexity.
